description_long: This is a heavy-duty sledgehammer featuring a robust, rectangular black metal head with chamfered edges and a central circular end cap. The head is solid and appears to be forged from steel, showing a matte finish with some subtle surface texture. The handle is long, straight, and cylindrical, constructed from wood with a weathered, grayish appearance, and it is securely fitted into the hammer head. The handle's end is slightly rounded and shows a natural wood grain, while the grip area is wrapped or coated in a gray material, possibly for improved handling. The hammer is utilitarian in design, with no visible branding or decorative elements. It is suitable for demanding tasks such as breaking concrete or driving stakes in urban construction or maintenance settings.
description: A heavy sledgehammer with a black metal head and a long, straight, weathered wooden handle, featuring a gray grip area. The tool is rugged and utilitarian, ideal for heavy-duty urban tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight wooden handle and the rectangular black metal head.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the broad striking face of the hammer head and the circular end cap.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's length and the other side of the hammer head.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the end of the handle and the back face of the hammer head.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a gray, possibly rubberized, grip area; cylindrical and slightly rounded at the end, showing visible wood grain and weathering.
attribute_head_type: Rectangular sledgehammer head made of black steel, with chamfered edges and a broad, flat striking face; no claws or peen features.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible weathering and discoloration on the handle; minor surface texture and scuffing on the hammer head.
